title: aphanes arvensis subsp. maroccana
intro: Aphanes arvensis subsp. maroccana, commonly known as the lesser plantain or buck's horn plantain, is a subspecies of plantain found in Morocco and is a member of the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aphanes arvensis subsp. maroccana include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
